Identify patterns in localization bugs, analyze systemic issues, and proactively propose upstream workflow improvements to optimize translation memories, style guides, and glossaries. Oversee testing coverage across a wide matrix of devices, ensuring consistent UI layout, font rendering, and functional behavior across iOS, Android, Web, and OTT (Over-the-Top) platforms. Advocate forculturally relevant content and inclusive experiences in all localized products. REQUIREMENTS Possess 4+ years of hands-on experience in localization QA, linguistic testing, internationalization (i18n), or global release validation, ideally within a fast-paced digital media, streaming, or consumer technology environment. Demonstrate a deep understanding of functional QA testing methodologies, test plan execution, regression testing, and the overall software development lifecycle (SDLC) across mobile, web, and smart device applications. Proven experience executing, coordinating, or managing complex linguistic quality assurance reviews across diverse international markets, successfully handling high volumes of content across multiple languages and locales. Apply a sophisticated understanding of cultural sensitivities, regional regulatory nuances, local adaptation best practices, and inclusive language guidelines to ensure all digital media content is locally relevant and brand-compliant. Showcase exceptional written and verbal communication skills, characterized by a meticulous eye for linguistic precision, user int

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
jobs.description: Lumos is looking for a full-time Enterprise Account Executive ! In this role, you'll drive revenue through new business acquisitions, account expansion, and customer retention with complex enterprise accounts. The Enterprise Account Executive will build relationships at all levels within the customer's organization, including the C-suite, while understanding their unique needs and goals. This role will be a critical part of Lumos ’ enterprise sales team. The Enterprise Account Executive will specialize in building and nurturing relationships with customers while understanding their challenges and delivering impactful strategies that align with their business objectives. Company Summary Lumos is on a mission to deliver lightning-fast, 100% Fiber Optic internet to the communities that need it most. After the merger of Lumos Networks and North State in 2022, we quickly rose to become a leading fiber internet provider. And as of April 1, 2025, we’ve joined forces with T-Mobile to launch T-Fiber—a joint venture that blends Lumos ’ infrastructure expertise with T-Mobile’s national reach to bring fiber to more homes than ever before. We currently connect over 475,000 homes, businesses, and multi-family buildings across nine states in the East and Midwest. With major growth in markets like Ohio, Alabama, Florida, and Georgia—and a shared goal of reaching 12 to 15 million homes by 2030— Lumos is playing a vital role in closing the digital divide. Our Mission and Values At Lumos , we are driven by our commitment to a brighter tomorrow . We take pride in being the first to deliver 100% Fiber Optic Internet for families, small businesses, and communities. We know the possibilities of tomorrow can’t be built on the infrastructure of yesterday. And when we deliver uninterrupted Internet and limitless capacity, we create new opportunities for everyone. We believe that the past is over, and the future is fast. That’s why we are who we are: the disruptors of the status quo. Guided by our purpose and startup mindset , we step up, move first, and adapt as we go. Here, there’s no hierarchy, only hustle. We are all servant leaders , confident in our purpose and humble enough to pay close attention to the details. We all have the power to help solve problems and find solutions for our customers and teammates - and we do - by putting people first and doing whatever it takes to build loyalty with our customers and within our team. We’re passionate about our customers’ experience . Their futures don’t stand still, and neither do ours. We’ll never stop reinventing ourselves to meet our customers’ evolving needs - because we know that our work, delivering 100% fiber optic internet, truly changes people’s lives. Essential Functions Practices strategic account management by developing and executing account plans to achieve sales targets, identify growth opportunities, and expand the company's footprint within existing accounts. Cultivates strong relationships with key contacts within enterprise accounts, acting as a trusted advisor, while building rapport at all levels of the customer’s organization. Identifies and pursues new business opportunities within the enterprise market, often involving hunting for new leads and prospects. Identifies new opportunities by leading the sales process from initial discovery through close, managing complex negotiations and ensuring alignment with client needs and company objectives. Drives customer satisfaction and loyalty by ensuring successful implementation and adoption of solutions, and by addressing any concerns or issues that may arise. Generates revenue by achieving or exceeding sales targets, driving pipeline growth, and maximizing revenue within assigned accounts. Works effectively with internal teams, including product, marketing, and engineering, to ensure alignment and deliver a cohesive customer experience. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Required Requires 6+ years of experience in national and enterprise

jobs.title: Experienced Spanish Interpreter  (LATAM)
jobs.description: About Us Founded in 2020, Kreato Global is a premier BPO company specializing in comprehensive language solutions. We are a recognized leader in high-quality medical interpretation, providing seamless Over-the-Phone Interpretation (OPI) and Video Remote Interpretation (VRI) services that bridge vital communication gaps in the global healthcare sector. The Role We are seeking a skilled, highly motivated English-Spanish Interpreter to join our 100% remote team in Latin America. Initially focused on OPI, this role offers a clear pathway to advance into VRI. You will be responsible for providing accurate, consecutive interpretation primarily in medical settings, as well as facilitating crucial communication in social services and financial contexts. We accept valid certifications from EF SET (4-Skill), TOEFL, or CAE. Medical Interpretation Certification: You must provide proof of medical interpreter knowledge issued within the last 12 months. Accepted certifications include: ALTA Spanish fluency: Minimum score of 9 required. LanguageStat (PICSI): Passing score of 75–80% required. Core Skills: Complete mastery of medical and customer service terminology. Compliance: Must currently hold, or be willing to obtain, HIPAA and FWA (Fraud, Waste, and Abuse) certifications. Background Check: Willingness to successfully complete a standard background check via email. Technical & Workspace Requirements Internet Connection: A hardwired Ethernet connection directly to your router is mandatory (Wi-Fi is strictly prohibited). Hardware: A high-quality, noise-canceling USB headset. Computer Specs: Minimum of Windows 10 OS and 4GB RAM. Browser: The latest version of Google Chrome, Apple Safari, or Microsoft Edge. Workspace: A dedicated, quiet, and fully HIPAA-compliant working environment. What We Offer Competitive Compensation: Attractive pay based strictly on interpreted minutes. Schedule Flexibility: Set your own working hours based on your availability (subject to a minimum weekly hour requirement). Work from Anywhere: A 100% remote position allowing you to work from the comfort of your home in LATAM. Clear Career Progression: Opportunities to transition from OPI to VRI based on performance (which includes a higher per-minute rate), with the potential to build a long-term career and join our core staff. jobs.title: Fullstack Developer SR | Angular + Node.js
jobs.description: Somos redbee , una empresa argentina de tecnología con más de 14 años de experiencia redefiniendo la forma de construir productos digitales en la industria financiera. Nos apasiona co-crear soluciones innovadoras junto a nuestros clientes. Combinamos experiencia, calidad y escalabilidad para desarrollar productos estratégicos y de misión crítica. Nos destaca nuestra obsesión por aportar valor rápido y de calidad. ¿Cuáles serán tus responsabilidades? Diseñar y desarrollar soluciones asegurando escalabilidad, resiliencia y alto rendimiento. Implementar aplicaciones frontend con Angular 20 en adelante, aplicando buenas prácticas y clean architecture. Diseñar e implementar arquitecturas basadas en microservicios y BFF con Node.js y Nest.js, asegurando eficiencia y confiabilidad. Liderar iniciativas, coordinar entregas y colaborar activamente con el cliente para alinear los objetivos del proyecto con las necesidades del negocio. ¿Qué esperamos de ti? Más de 6 años de experiencia dominando Angular 20 o versiones recientes, junto con TypeScript. Desarrollo de aplicaciones backend con Node.js y NestJS. Diseño, implementación y mantenimiento de APIs HTTP/REST. Conocimientos de arquitecturas basadas en microservicios y BFF. Uso de Docker. Es deseable: Conocimientos de arquitecturas de microfrontends. Despliegue y operación de aplicaciones en Kubernetes. Conocimientos de Kong u otros API gateways. Prácticas de Spec-Driven Development. ¿Cuál es nuestra propuesta de valor? En redbee buscamos crear un espacio de trabajo donde las personas se sientan felices y puedan desarrollarse; por eso ofrecemos 10 días hábiles de vacaciones y adicionamos 5 días hábiles más. Trabajamos con flexibilidad y de forma remote-first y, si vivís en AMBA, nos encontramos una vez al mes en la oficina para compartir, charlar y seguir construyendo vínculos. Sí estás fuera de AMBA el trabajo es full remote. Nos interesa tu comodidad, es por eso que contamos con coworks y brindamos equipamiento para tu espacio de trabajo. Además brindamos un monto mensual de puntos para canjear en una plataforma de beneficios. Sabemos que la familia ocupa un lugar importante: ofrecemos prepaga para vos y tu grupo familiar. También otorgamos licencias extendidas para que puedas estar presente en esos momentos que importan. Creemos en el aprendizaje continuo y en el crecimiento a largo plazo. Vas a tener clases de inglés, acceso a plataformas de capacitación y un plan de desarrollo claro, pensado para acompañar tu viaje dentro de redbee . En redbee buscamos dejar huella en las trayectorias profesionales y ser una experiencia memorable en tu carrera. Creemos que la calidad técnica y la humana van de la mano, y desde ahí transformamos realidades a través de la tecnología. Si queres ser parte, ¡queremos conocerte! Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Lighting Artist - GameSim - Talent Pipeline
jobs.description: GameSim is a game development studio that partners with clients to build games across PC/console and a range of art styles. We care about craftsmanship, collaboration, and shipping work we’re proud to put our names on. If you love building environments and believable spaces we’d like to meet you. Our headquarters is based in Orlando, FL! Talent Pipeline Posting Notice Please note that this posting is not tied to an immediate vacancy. We are proactively building a pipeline of qualified candidates in anticipation of potential hiring needs over the coming months. Applications will be reviewed and retained for future consideration as opportunities arise. We are seeking a Lighting Artist to create high-quality real-time lighting and help support the execution of lighting work across GameSim projects. This is a hands-on production role with opportunities to take on project-level leadership responsibilities. In addition to completing assigned lighting work, you may help coordinate tasks, review the work of other artists, provide constructive feedback, and ensure scenes meet the visual and technical quality bar established by Art Direction and the client. You will collaborate with Art Directors, Environment Artists, Technical Artists, VFX Artists, Designers, Engineers, Producers, clients, and partner studios to translate creative direction into lighting solutions that support mood, gameplay readability, environmental storytelling, and performance. What Success Looks Like Within your first 90 days, you will: Develop a clear understanding of the project’s visual direction, lighting pipeline, gameplay requirements, technical constraints, and production schedule. Build effective working relationships with Art Direction, Production, Technical Art, Environment Art, Design, and other project disciplines. Create polished lighting work that meets the project’s established visual and technical quality bar. Take ownership of lighting for assigned scenes, environments, sequences, or gameplay areas. Provide useful feedback and support to other artists when asked to review or coordinate lighting work. Identify artistic, technical, or production risks early and communicate them to the appropriate project leads. Deliver assigned work on schedule and respond effectively to internal and client feedback. Responsibilities Lighting Creation and Execution Create high-quality real-time lighting for gameplay environments, levels, cinematics, characters, and interactive sequences. Translate art direction, concept art, reference material, gameplay requirements, narrative goals, and client feedback into effective lighting solutions. Use lighting to establish mood, atmosphere, visual hierarchy, environmental storytelling, and player focus. Ensure lighting supports gameplay readability, navigation, combat visibility, character presentation, and important interactive elements. Develop and refine exposure, color, contrast, fog, atmosphere, reflections, shadows, emissive response, color grading, and post-processing. Create lighting for a range of visual styles, including realistic, stylized, cinematic, and highly art-directed projects. Maintain visual consistency across related environments, gameplay spaces, sequences, times of day, and lighting conditions. Bring assigned scenes from initial lighting setup through final polish. Respond to feedback from Art Direction, project leadership, and clients in a timely and professional manner. Ensure completed work meets project requirements for quality, performance, organization, and delivery. Leadership and Quality Support Help oversee lighting work for an assigned scene, group of scenes, feature, or milestone when requested by project leadership. Review the work of other lighting artists or multidisciplinary artists contributing to lighting. Provide clear, respectful, and actionable feedback through written notes, live reviews, reference images, paintovers, or in-engine examples. Help ensure lighting work follows approved

jobs.title: Social Compliance Lead Auditor
jobs.description: Position Summary: The core responsibility of the Consumer Products Assurance Social Compliance Lead Auditor will be to execute assigned social compliance audits for Eurofins ’ clients – primarily in the US. As a lead auditor, the role will involve managing and developing social compliance auditors – in addition to leading audit executions. Objectives: Audit Execution Lead auditor will support the operating results of the business through the execution of audit services – with approximately 14 billable days per month. In audit execution, the lead auditor will be responsible to for supporting the training and development of junior auditors – participating in audits as a team member. Technical Support and Governance As a member of the audit team, the lead auditor will contribute technical input and perspective to the development of operating procedures and methodologies to ensure audit service delivery capabilities support client service delivery objectives - enhancing satisfaction among clients. Customer Service Provide customer support as the audit service stream program managers for selected large global accounts and collaborative programs. Roles and Responsibilities: Audit Execution and Reporting Perform audits consistent with Eurofins ’ client and program instructions in accordance with established protocols Prepare audit reports in a complete, accurate and timely manner for submission to the Eurofins ARCC Respond timely to any post audit queries from ARCC to close and finalize audit report Technical Support and Governance Ensure the integrity and consistency of the delivery of CPA audit services though support for the continuous improvement of operating procedures and methodologies to ensure service delivery capabilities support client service delivery objectives on a global basis. Provide subject matter support for the effective and timely resolution of issues and problems with audits related in other key sourcing markets. Bachelors or advanced degree in Business, Finance or a related field of study. At least 5 years' experience in professional services and/or auditing. At least 3years’ experience managing teams of professionals. APSCA CSCA certificate/license Key Skills Subject Matter Significant knowledge of audit subject matter – particularly social audits, service delivery methodologies for audit services and the demonstrated ability to implement them General knowledge of risk management, auditing, and process management consulting concepts and the demonstrated ability to implement them Client interaction Demonstrated ability to work with clients and facilities to be audited, understand their requirements, define and assist in solving problems, and generally ensure client’s total satisfaction with Eurofins Management/Leadership Effective oral and written communications skills including professional English presentation Strong skills as a team leader and ability to work well with peers. Highly developed organizational and interpersonal skills. Ability to interpret regulations and standards. Technical aptitude, the ability to quickly assimilate and assess new information and develop plans to capitalize or react to it Schedule: 40 hours per week What we offer: Excellent full time benefits including comprehensive medical coverage, dental, and vision options Life and disability insurance 401(k) with company match Paid vacation and holidays Eurofins USA Consumer Product Testing is a Disabled and Veteran Equal Employment Opportunity employer. Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins works with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and labelling is accurate. jobs.title: Legal Coordinator
jobs.description: For over 30 years, Gersh Autism has empowered students on the autism spectrum to reach their full potential. Serving individuals aged 5-21 across the U.S. and Puerto Rico, our network of K-12 schools provides personalized education tailored to each student’s unique learning style. We offer comprehensive therapeutic services—speech, physical, occupational therapy, and more—along with vocational training for older students. Our dedicated team collaborates with families to ensure academic, social, and emotional growth, preparing students for success beyond the classroom. At Gersh Autism , we are committed to helping individuals on the autism spectrum lead fulfilling, independent lives. Gersh Autism is seeking an experienced Legal Coordinator to support student placement, enrollment, and legal compliance across our programs serving students on the autism spectrum. This role works closely with Admissions, School Leadership, and external legal partners to ensure placement decisions are timely, compliant, and aligned with special education law and organizational standards. Key Responsibilities Student Placement & Legal Compliance Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local special education laws, including IDEA and FAPE Provide legal and procedural guidance throughout the admissions, placement, and enrollment process Review student records, including IEPs, evaluations, related services, and transportation mandates, to support placement decisions Documentation & Case Management Prepare, issue, track, and manage enrollment contracts, amendments, and Ten-Day Notices, ensuring accuracy and timeliness Maintain complete, accurate, and audit-ready student files in accordance with legal and organizational standards Collaboration & Coordination Partner with Admissions, School Leadership, Transportation, Finance, and School Operations in a remote environment to support placement and enrollment needs Collaborate with special education attorneys, advocates, and school districts regarding referrals, placements, and due process matters Qualifications Legal, admissions, or compliance experience within a special education or related educational setting required Experience working with both referral and non-referral schools; ABA school experience strongly preferred Strong working knowledge of IEPs, evaluations, and special education documentation Familiarity with IDEA, FAPE, and the Impartial Hearing process Knowledge of the NYC and/or Long Island special education landscape preferred Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree or paralegal certification a plus Benefits Gersh Autism offers a competitive compensation package, including comprehensive health and retirement benefits, professional development opportunities, and a supportive work environment committed to your personal and professional growth. Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision) Retirement Plan (401k matching) Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D) Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity) Short-Term & Long-Term Disability Training & Development Compensation includes base and KRA Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
jobs.description: About Huzzle At Huzzle , we connect exceptional talent with top opportunities at leading companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include fast-growing startups, digital agencies, SaaS companies, and tech-enabled businesses across industries such as MarTech, FinTech, EdTech, and eCommerce. Unlike traditional outsourcing agencies, we place you directly with innovative companies where you become a valued member of the team. About the Company Our client is an established digital marketing agency based in Colorado, specializing in high-impact search engine optimization (SEO) and comprehensive online growth strategies. With over 15 years of experience, the team serves a diverse range of industries, including legal, medical, and B2B sectors, by providing results-driven services such as paid ad management, web development, and review marketing. They focus on transparency and data-backed reporting to help businesses across the United States scale their online visibility and capture high-quality leads. Job Summary This is a high-impact opportunity for an experienced digital marketing professional to move beyond project management and become a true strategic partner for a diverse portfolio of clients. In this role, you will serve as the primary advisor, turning complex data into actionable growth strategies. You will join a team focused on innovation and measurable results, enjoying a clear path to leadership while helping businesses across the country scale through data-driven insights. Key Responsibilities Serve as the strategic leader and primary point of contact for a client portfolio from onboarding through long-term growth. Develop and oversee comprehensive digital marketing strategies across SEO, paid media, local search, and analytics. Monitor campaign performance using GA4, Google Search Console, and SEMrush to identify trends and address challenges. Present high-level recommendations and reports that translate marketing data into clear business outcomes. Collaborate with internal digital marketing specialists to ensure campaign execution remains aligned with client business goals. Proactively identify new opportunities for service expansion or strategic pivots to maximize client ROI. Foster long-term, trust-based relationships by acting as a consultant rather than just a task manager. Requirements 2+ years of client-facing experience at a digital marketing agency, specifically managing account strategy and performance. Proven ability to develop and present digital marketing strategies that drive measurable growth. Working knowledge of the digital marketing ecosystem, including GA4, Google Search Console, Google Business Profile, SEMrush, Google Ads, Meta Business Manager, and WordPress. Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn technical data into a compelling narrative for clients. Exceptional communication skills and the confidence to explain complex concepts in simple, actionable terms. Precise organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Availability to work during core business hours of 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Mountain Time. Nice-to-Haves Experience working with clients in healthcare or other highly regulated industries. Advanced proficiency in data visualization or reporting software. Benefits 🤝 Collaborative team environment with structured onboarding and support 💰 Compensation: Based on skillset and experience 🌎 100% remote role with global exposure 🚀 Fast-growing companies and performance-based career acceleration 🎯 Real impact in high-growth environments Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: ML Infrastructure Engineer
jobs.description: ML Infrastructure Engineer - Remote Bright Vision Technologies is a technology consulting and software development company delivering cloud, AI, data, and enterprise solutions across the United States. This is a fantastic opportunity to join an established and well-respected organization offering tremendous career growth potential. Job Title: ML Infrastructure Engineer Location: 100% Remote (U.S.) Position Type: Full-time, Direct W2 Salary Range: $100,000–$150,000 Annually Experience Required: 6+ years Sponsorship: U.S. Citizens, Green Card Holders, EAD Holders, and H-1B transfer candidates are encouraged to apply. We are unable to sponsor new H-1B visa petitions for this position. Job Summary We are seeking an AI Infrastructure Engineer to design, build, and operate the platform layer that powers large-scale AI training and inference workloads. The role focuses on GPU clusters, distributed training frameworks, scheduling, storage performance, and developer experience for ML engineers and researchers, with strong emphasis on reliability, efficiency, and cost control. The ideal candidate has built or operated production AI infrastructure at scale, understands the interaction between hardware, kernel, scheduler, and ML framework, and brings strong software engineering discipline to platform work. Key Responsibilities Design and operate GPU and accelerator infrastructure for training and inference, spanning on-prem clusters, cloud-managed services, and hybrid configurations. Build scheduling, queueing, and resource-sharing systems that maximize accelerator utilization across many teams. Integrate frameworks such as PyTorch, JAX, DeepSpeed, FSDP, Megatron-LM, and Ray Train into a unified platform offering. Operate high-performance storage systems and data pipelines that keep accelerators fed with training data at near-line-rate. Design networking architectures supporting RDMA, InfiniBand, NCCL, and high-bandwidth collective communication. Build observability for AI workloads including utilization, throughput, training stability, and failure-mode analytics. Implement checkpointing, restart, and fault-tolerance patterns for long-running training jobs at scale. Drive cost optimization across compute, storage, and networking through scheduling, spot capacity, and right-sizing. Develop developer tooling and paved-road workflows that let researchers launch experiments safely and efficiently. Partner with research and applied ML teams to plan capacity for upcoming training runs. Implement security controls, isolation, and access management for multi-tenant AI infrastructure. Drive automation across cluster provisioning, lifecycle management, and configuration enforcement. Maintain runbooks, capacity dashboards, and operational documentation for the AI platform. Stay current with AI infrastructure research, accelerator hardware, and emerging open-source AI tooling. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field. Six or more years of experience in infrastructure, platform, or HPC engineering. Hands-on experience operating GPU clusters or large-scale ML training infrastructure. Strong proficiency in Python and at least one systems language such as Go or C++. Deep understanding of distributed training, accelerator architectures, and collective communication. Experience with Kubernetes, Slurm, Ray, or similar scheduling systems for ML workloads. Strong understanding of Linux internals, networking, and high-performance storage. Experience with at least one major cloud provider’s ML infrastructure offerings. Strong software engineering practices including testing, CI/CD, and code review. Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills. Preferred Qualifications Experience operating InfiniBand or RDMA networking at scale. Contributions to open-source ML infrastructure projects. Familiarity with custom orchestrators or research-grade training stacks. Exposure to frontier model trainin

We look forward to collaborating with you! Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Environmental Engineer
jobs.description: About Edged Data Centers Edged is a fast-growing provider of sustainable data centers and energy infrastructure. The company builds and operates a global network of ultra-efficient, AI-ready data centers with waterless cooling for colocation, hyperscale and build-to-suit customers. The highly differentiated infrastructure platform is the first large-scale, global deployment of waterless cooling platform-wide and transforms data center growth into a catalyst for sustainable development. Edged is backed by a deep proprietary technology stack that will include renewable microgrid systems, ultra-clean generators, and waste-to-fuel solutions. The platform is designed for rapid deployment with more than a dozen new data centers operating or under construction across Europe and North America and a gigawatt-scale project pipeline. The North American division of Edged is a joint venture between one of the largest private multinational corporations in the world and an affiliate of Endeavour. Endeavour is a purpose-driven organization helping innovators to develop and launch breakthrough solutions for global challenges in the areas of energy, water, and waste. Endeavour companies are made up of passionate change-makers who want to leave the world better than the way we found it. Today, Endeavour is deploying solutions that are immediately scalable and deliver both high financial returns and positive impact, including EV charging, waterless data center cooling, and advanced energy systems. Opportunity We're seeking an experienced Environmental Engineer to lead air permitting and environmental compliance for edge and large-scale data center projects. This role combines regulatory permitting expertise with environmental compliance to keep development timelines on track while meeting all state and federal requirements for Edged Datacenters in the United States. Responsibilities Manage air permitting processes for data center facilities, including permits for backup generators, emergency engines, and related emission sources Prepare and submit permit applications to state and federal agencies; track through approval Lead environmental permitting for new site development, from initial site assessment through permit issuance Oversee and manage third-party consultants and contractors performing permitting work, ensuring quality, accuracy, and on-time submittals Ensure ongoing compliance with air, water, and waste regulations across project lifecycle Support field operations during construction and commissioning to maintain environmental compliance Support environmental compliance audits and manage corrective actions Coordinate wildlife, water, and waste management requirements as needed for site development Interface with regulators, project managers, and contractors Qualifications Bachelor of Science in Environmental Engineering or related field 10+ years of environmental compliance experience, ideally in energy, oil & gas, or infrastructure Demonstrated air permitting expertise (Title V, minor source, PSD/NSR familiarity a plus) Working knowledge of state and federal waste, water, and air regulations Field experience supporting operations and compliance Strong background in compliance auditing Experience managing outside consultants or contractors on permitting deliverables and environmental compliance Ability to manage multiple permits and deadlines across sites Preferred Experience with generator/engine air permitting Multi-state permitting experience Familiarity with data center or industrial facility development EEO Statement: Edged is an equal opportunities employer. We believe in ensuring equal access to employment opportunities for all. We assess candidate qualifications and make recruiting decisions based on the experience, capabilities and skills you share with us in your application and applicable materials. Hiring and employment decisions within Edged are not based on religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sex

jobs.title: Listing and Distribution Admin
jobs.description: About the Company: The client operates and optimizes short-term rentals to create ROI for homeowners and unforgettable vacation homes for guests. Based in Orlando, Florida, the client offers short-term rentals across the United States, Canada, and South America. Our team of 160+ employees manages 400+ properties on behalf of owners. We treat every property in our portfolio as an investment with a focus on maximizing profit without sacrificing best-in-class hospitality for our guests. Job Overview: The Listing Administrator is responsible for the end-to-end management of property listings across multiple short-term rental platforms. This includes onboarding new listings, ensuring listing accuracy and optimization, distributing content across OTAs, managing listing-related guest reviews and disputes, and maintaining listing compliance and reporting. The role requires strong attention to detail, problem-solving skills, and coordination with both internal teams and external platforms to ensure high-quality, high-performing listings. Key Responsibilities: Gather and build listings by inputting property information into the Property Management System (PMS), ensuring complete and accurate data across platforms such as Airbnb, Booking.com , and VRBO. Monitor and maintain high-quality listing content—including photos, descriptions, amenities, and policies—while coordinating with the Marketing and Content teams as needed. Optimize listing performance and health by implementing strategic edits and seasonal updates to maximize visibility and conversions. Respond to guest reviews, initiate disputes where applicable, and uphold review quality in line with established standards. Prepare and maintain reports on listing and account activity, performance metrics, ratings, guest feedback, and compliance (as applicable). Troubleshoot and resolve distribution issues with OTAs to ensure listings remain online, bookable, and properly aligned across all channels. Assist in the launch and integration of new OTA accounts and manage the full lifecycle of listings, including launching, updating, and relaunching. Support licensing and compliance processes by preparing required documentation and ensuring adherence to local regulations. Collaborate with departments such as Revenue, Operations, and Marketing to ensure listing accuracy and strategic consistency. Provide administrative and project-based support to the Listings & Distribution Leads and contribute to ongoing process improvements and documentation. Requirements Highly Organized: Efficiently manages multiple tasks and priorities. Detail-Oriented: Thorough in analyzing data and handling tasks. Time Management: Effectively executes tasks and meets deadlines. Analytical Skills: Skilled in evaluating trends and making data-driven decisions. Tech-Savvy: Proficient with tech tools, including Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets. Effective Communicator: Strong in written and verbal communication. Remote Work Experience: Productive in a remote team environment. Tool Proficiency: Experience with Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and Slack is a plus. Passionate: Interested in vacation rentals, real estate, and tech startups. Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in business, hospitality, or related field (preferred). Relevant Experience: 2+ years in customer service or administrative roles in STR or hotel settings. Structured Roles: Prefers roles with defined responsibilities. Extra Awesome: Familiarity with Airbnb, Booking.com , VRBO, and/or other short-term rental platforms is an asset; Background in the short-term rental or hotel industry is preferred Proficient with Guesty or other Property Management/Channel Manager Software is an asset; Available to start ASAP Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Experienced Spanish Interpreter  (LATAM)
jobs.description: About Us Founded in 2020, Kreato Global is a premier BPO company specializing in comprehensive language solutions. We are a recognized leader in high-quality medical interpretation, providing seamless Over-the-Phone Interpretation (OPI) and Video Remote Interpretation (VRI) services that bridge vital communication gaps in the global healthcare sector. The Role We are seeking a skilled, highly motivated English-Spanish Interpreter to join our 100% remote team in Latin America. Initially focused on OPI, this role offers a clear pathway to advance into VRI. You will be responsible for providing accurate, consecutive interpretation primarily in medical settings, as well as facilitating crucial communication in social services and financial contexts. Qualifications & Experience Experience: 6 months to 1 year minimum of professional experience in remote medical interpretation. English Proficiency: Minimum C1 fluency. We accept valid certifications from EF SET (4-Skill), TOEFL, or CAE. Medical Interpretation Certification: You must provide proof of medical interpreter knowledge issued within the last 12 months. Accepted certifications include: ALTA Spanish fluency: Minimum score of 9 required. LanguageStat (PICSI): Passing score of 75–80% required. Core Skills: Complete mastery of medical and customer service terminology. Compliance: Must currently hold, or be willing to obtain, HIPAA and FWA (Fraud, Waste, and Abuse) certifications. Background Check: Willingness to successfully complete a standard background check via email. Technical & Workspace Requirements Internet Connection: A hardwired Ethernet connection directly to your router is mandatory (Wi-Fi is strictly prohibited). Hardware: A high-quality, noise-canceling USB headset. Computer Specs: Minimum of Windows 10 OS and 4GB RAM. Browser: The latest version of Google Chrome, Apple Safari, or Microsoft Edge. Workspace: A dedicated, quiet, and fully HIPAA-compliant working environment. What We Offer Competitive Compensation: Attractive pay based strictly on interpreted minutes. Schedule Flexibility: Set your own working hours based on your availability (subject to a minimum weekly hour requirement). Work from Anywhere: A 100% remote position allowing you to work from the comfort of your home in LATAM. Clear Career Progression: Opportunities to transition from OPI to VRI based on performance (which includes a higher per-minute rate), with the potential to build a long-term career and join our core staff. Supportive Culture: Become part of a friendly, expert, and highly supportive global team. Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Senior Accountant (German Speaker)
jobs.description: This role is part of our Central Finance Team at saas.group "> saas.group . What is saas.group ? Freedom and autonomy: We’re a high-trust team, and you’ll be given lots of flexibility to solve problems in your own way — with plenty of help from the team when you need it. jobs.source_id: supportyourapp-(Fluent Ukrainian & English) Senior AI Solutions Architect (EU, remotely)
jobs.title: (Fluent Ukrainian & English) Senior AI Solutions Architect (EU, remotely)
jobs.description: Who are we? SupportYourApp is an international Intelligent Support-as-a-Service company that has been providing business process outsourcing services to other IT companies around the globe (technical and customer support, services to improve customer experience) for the past 15 years. We have 1500+ people in our international community. We operate globally, supporting clients in 60 languages, partnering with industry leaders like MasterCard, Calm and MacPaw. With international hubs and coworking spaces around the world, we also develop innovative products like Quidget and improve the customer experience every single day. About the role: We’re looking for a Senior AI Solutions Architect to lead and accelerate our AI transformation journey. This is a highly strategic and hands-on role where you’ll turn ideas into real, revenue-generating solutions. You’ll combine a founder’s mindset with strong commercial ownership — driving execution, testing ideas through pilots, and building scalable AI-powered services. You will work cross-functionally with Sales, Product, and Delivery to ensure innovation directly impacts business growth. Excited? Let’s see what it takes 💛 What you will do: Strategy Execution & Acceleration: Take ownership of the existing AI transformation strategy and drive its execution at pace; Identify gaps, dependencies, and bottlenecks, resolving them proactively; Continuously refine strategy based on market feedback, client needs, and real-world learnings. Product & Solution Development: Contribute to building AI-powered service offerings beyond headcount-based models; Prototype, test, and scale solutions from proof-of-concept to client deployment; Define reusable AI components (knowledge bases, copilots, QA automation, intelligent routing). Commercial Impact & Revenue Ownership: Own revenue targets for innovation-driven offerings; Partner with Sales and Business Growth teams to position AI solutions in client conversations; Develop pricing and packaging strategies for hybrid AI + human service models. Process Building & Operational Design: Build repeatable frameworks for evaluating, piloting, and scaling new solutions; Establish workflows, governance, and reporting for the innovation function; Define and track success metrics (e.g., speed to pilot, adoption, cost efficiency, revenue impact). Internal Transformation: Drive implementation of AI agents into internal workflows; Partner with internal teams to identify automation opportunities that improve efficiency and margins. jobs.title: Frontend Developer (Magento)
jobs.description: Por qué Keyrus , por qué ahora! Keyrus es un grupo internacional con 2.800 consultores y expertos en 28 países, construido sobre una convicción: la IA no transforma las empresas por sí sola. La inteligencia con arquitectura sí. Durante más de 30 años, hemos construido las bases de datos, gobierno, procesos y sistemas que hacen posible que la inteligencia funcione en la operación real del negocio. En Keyrus , no nos enfocamos en pilotos aislados. Ayudamos a las organizaciones a llevar la IA a producción, integrarla en sus procesos y convertirla en resultados sostenibles: operacionalizamos la inteligencia. La IA no reemplaza a las personas. Las reposiciona donde más valor generan: entendiendo, decidiendo, diseñando y creando. En este rol, no solo desarrollarás habilidades técnicas. También fortalecerás tu criterio, tu capacidad de arquitectura y tu forma de resolver problemas reales con clientes. Con el tiempo, podrás crecer como uno de esos perfiles que conectan datos, IA y toma de decisiones humanas a escala, trabajando con diferentes industrias, equipos y geografías. Este no es solo un rol que ocupar. Es una disciplina que desarrollar y una historia que ayudarás a construir, como parte de los equipos que hacen de Keyrus un Arquitecto de Inteligencia. La tecnología amplifica. La cultura de Keyrus marca la diferencia. El rigor operativo conecta ambas para convertir la inteligencia en impacto real. Qué harás en este rol: Como Frontend Developer (Magento) , serás responsable de asegurar la evolución, estabilidad y mantenibilidad de la capa de presentación de plataformas e-commerce, desarrollando interfaces modernas, responsivas y de alto desempeño que impactan directamente la experiencia del usuario y la continuidad operativa del negocio. Desarrollar y mantener interfaces web para plataformas Magento, asegurando componentes reutilizables, escalables y alineados con los requerimientos del proyecto. Implementar nuevas funcionalidades frontend, garantizando una experiencia de usuario consistente, accesible y responsive. Participar en el desarrollo de temas, componentes y personalizaciones dentro del ecosistema Magento. Ejecutar mantenimiento evolutivo y correctivo sobre la capa frontend, atendiendo incidentes, ajustes funcionales y mejoras continuas. Corregir errores y optimizar componentes existentes para mejorar el rendimiento, la usabilidad y la experiencia del usuario final. Colaborar con desarrolladores backend, QA, analistas funcionales y otros miembros del equipo para implementar soluciones integrales. Gestionar y versionar cambios mediante Git, siguiendo buenas prácticas de integración y control de versiones. Consumir APIs y servicios para integrar funcionalidades dinámicas dentro de la interfaz de usuario. Aplicar buenas prácticas de desarrollo frontend, incluyendo clean code, reutilización de componentes, documentación técnica y mantenibilidad. Participar en pruebas y validación de desarrollos para asegurar entregas funcionales y listas para despliegue. Acompañar procesos de transferencia de conocimiento hacia equipos internos, promoviendo continuidad operativa y adopción técnica. Qué esperamos de ti: Que combines criterio técnico con una ejecución consistente en contextos de evolución y mantenimiento de plataformas digitales. Que tengas una mentalidad orientada a resolver problemas reales con enfoque práctico y atención a la experiencia del usuario. Que valores la calidad del código y entiendas su impacto en el desempeño, mantenibilidad y escalabilidad del frontend. Que puedas trabajar de forma autónoma sin perder alineación con estándares, tiempos y objetivos del equipo. Que seas una persona cuidadosa con el detalle visual y funcional al trabajar sobre plataformas activas. Que comuniques de forma clara avances, riesgos técnicos y decisiones de implementación. Que tengas disposición para colaborar en entornos dinámicos, con foco en continuidad, aprendizaje y mejora constante. Qué aportas: Formación y certificaciones: Form

jobs.source_id: cleardesk-llc-Scheduler (QA Experience Required) | Work From Home, Make a Real Impact
jobs.title: Scheduler (QA Experience Required) | Work From Home, Make a Real Impact
jobs.description: This is a remote position. Want a remote role where your skills bring structure to busy teams, and your work truly helps people? At ClearDesk, you’ll get the flexibility to work from home, the stability of long-term placement, and the chance to be part of something that actually matters. Our Story Starts with People Like You The two founders, best friends, were running businesses that needed great talent fast. So, they started building remote teams across countries like the Philippines, Colombia, and India. Not only did it work, it thrived. They realized that talent isn’t limited by geography. With today’s technology and high-speed internet, we’re able to build high-performing global teams that support businesses across the U.S. So ClearDesk was born. Today, ClearDesk helps U.S. businesses grow by building world-class remote teams. We don’t just connect talent; we manage the entire experience, from recruiting to retention. And while we help clients thrive, we also stay deeply committed to helping our remote team members build real, lasting careers that support their lives and their families. That’s where you come in. The Role: Scheduler (QA Experience Required) Imagine being the person who keeps everything running behind the scenes in a fast-moving home care setting. Someone’s shift just got canceled last minute. You’ve already found a replacement. A caregiver calls in sick. You’re already on the phone with the next available team member. You don’t just fill in gaps in the schedule. You create order. You create confidence. And for both caregivers and clients, you become the quiet force that keeps things on track. More importantly, the people you help serve are seniors who rely on consistent, compassionate care to stay safe and comfortable in their homes. Every shift you coordinate ensures someone’s parent, grandparent, or loved one receives the care they deserve. As a Home Care Scheduler, you’ll be assigned to a U.S.-based home care client, working closely with their operations team. You’ll manage caregiver schedules, communicate changes, and help keep both sides (caregivers and clients) informed, supported, and calm even when things get a little hectic. And when things are calm? You’ll help with admin work or pitch in on light recruitment tasks, keeping things moving and meaningful. What Your Days Might Look Like: Build and maintain up-to-date caregiver schedules across multiple counties Respond quickly when someone cancels a shift or doesn’t clock in Answer calls from clients and caregivers using the VoIP system Update records and case notes in WellSky (we’ll train you for it) Work with the ops team to flag issues and make sure staffing stays smooth Conduct regular check-ins and evaluations with caregivers Generate weekly reports and keep leadership in the loop Who We Think Will Thrive in This Role: You’ve worked in scheduling before or think you can rock it (bonus points for healthcare or home care experience) You’re calm under pressure and naturally organized You love solving problems on the fly You’re comfortable with software systems and remote communication You’ve built strong relationships with stakeholders, becoming a trusted partner they can rely on You take initiative, but you’re also a great team player You are willing to work in a US timezone schedule You can secure the required technical setup, including: At least a 720p HD webcam A noise-canceling headset A primary internet connection of at least 25 Mbps A backup laptop, backup internet device, and backup power source A main computer with at least Intel Core i5 (or equivalent) and 8 GB RAM A backup computer with at least Intel Core i3 and 4 GB RAM Why ClearDesk? Because we believe work should be meaningful, even from home. At ClearDesk, you’ll work with clients who respect your time and value your skills. You’ll join a remote-first team that actually supports you, and you’ll know that the work you’re doing helps real people, especially seniors, receive the care

This role is ideal for someone who is confident in managing their own workload, enjoys working across multiple clients, and has solid Australian business tax experience. Key Responsibilities: Prepare company and individual income tax returns. Prepare and lodge Business Activity Statements (BAS) and Instalment Activity Statements (IAS). Perform bookkeeping and bank reconciliations for multiple Australian clients. Process payroll and ensure compliance with Australian payroll requirements. Prepare working papers and supporting documentation for financial statements. Maintain accurate financial records using cloud-based accounting software. Assist senior accountants with month-end and year-end compliance activities. Communicate professionally with internal team members to ensure timely completion of client work. Manage multiple client files while meeting deadlines and maintaining a high standard of accuracy. Requirements Minimum 3 years' experience in an Australian public practice accounting firm. Strong experience preparing company and individual tax returns. Experience preparing BAS, IAS, bookkeeping, payroll, and financial statements. Proficiency in Xero across multiple client files. Experience using Active Work Papers or similar workpaper software is highly regarded. Familiarity with Carbon or similar practice management software is an advantage. Good understanding of Australian taxation and accounting principles. Excellent written and verbal English communication skills. Highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities in a remote environment. Work Arrangement & Expectations: This is a remote role that will be set up as an independent contractor engagement. Strong understanding of web-to-CRM integrations (specifically Salesforce). Strong grasp of front-end fundamentals (HTML, CSS) and how they impact site speed, Core Web Vitals, and responsive design. jobs.title: Senior Account Executive – Enterprise Industrial Software (m/w/d)
jobs.description: Stellenangebot: Senior Account Executive – Enterprise Industrial Software (m/w/d) Für unser Unternehmen, einen führenden SaaS-Enablement-Dienstleister , suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine/n engagierte/n und erfahrene/n Senior Account Executive – Enterprise Industrial Software (m/w/d) . SingularitySales ist bekannt für erstklassige Leistungen im Bereich Go-to-Market und der Skalierung innovativer Softwarelösungen. Wir legen besonderen Wert auf eine moderne Unternehmenskultur, die Offenheit, Innovation und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung fördert. Als Senior Account Executive (m/w/d) bist du eine zentrale Säule unseres Vertriebsteams im Bereich Enterprise Industrial Software und spielst eine entscheidende Rolle beim Aufbau und Closing strategischer Deals mit Großkunden aus dem Fertigungsumfeld. Dein Fokus liegt darauf, Kundenbedarfe präzise zu analysieren, maßgeschneiderte Softwarelösungen zu präsentieren und Entscheidungsträger bis zum erfolgreichen Vertragsabschluss zu begleiten. Dabei baust du langfristige Partnerschaften auf und positionierst das Produkt-Portfolio strategisch in deinem Marktbereich. Wenn du dich durch exzellente kommunikative Fähigkeiten, starkes Business-Verständnis im industriellen Umfeld und eine nachweisbare Erfolgsbilanz im Closing komplexer Enterprise-Deals auszeichnest, freuen wir uns auf deine Unterstützung. Dich erwartet eine anspruchsvolle Rolle mit hoher Verantwortung – und die Möglichkeit, deine Karriere auf das nächste Level zu bringen, eingebettet in ein dynamisches Umfeld, das lebenslanges Lernen, Entwicklung und gemeinsame Erfolge fördert. Sei Teil eines Teams, das mit Leidenschaft innovative Softwarelösungen vorantreibt, nachhaltige Kundenbeziehungen aufbaut und ambitionierte Wachstumsziele realisiert. Requirements Deine Mission: Als Senior Account Executive (m/w/d) bist du Teil unseres Outsourcing-Teams für einen führenden Anbieter industrieller Softwarelösungen. Deine Mission: den kompletten Sales-Cycle eigenverantwortlich zu steuern – von der präzisen Analyse der Kundenbedarfe über die Präsentation maßgeschneiderter Enterprise-Lösungen bis hin zum erfolgreichen Vertragsabschluss. Dabei agierst du als vertrauensvoller Partner im Namen unseres Kunden, baust langfristige Beziehungen mit Entscheidungsträgern auf und positionierst das Portfolio strategisch im Markt. Mit deinem Beitrag stellst du sicher, dass Wachstum messbar wird und die Sales-Exzellenz unseres Kunden nachhaltig gestärkt wird. Deine Aufgaben: Closing & Deal Ownership: Verantwortung für den gesamten Abschlussprozess – vom ersten Meeting (ausführliche Discovery und Business Case) bis hin zur erfolgreichen Vertragsunterzeichnung. Kundenberatung: Analyse komplexer Anforderungen aus dem Manufacturing- und Industrieumfeld, Präsentation maßgeschneiderter Softwarelösungen und Positionierung des Produktportfolios als strategischer Mehrwert. Beziehungsmanagement: Aufbau und Pflege langfristiger Partnerschaften mit Entscheidern und Key Accounts in Fertigungsunternehmen, um nachhaltiges Wachstum zu sichern. Vertriebsstrategie: Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Go-to-Market-Ansätzen im Namen des Kunden, mit Fokus auf Effizienz und Impact im industriellen Enterprise-Umfeld. CRM- & Pipeline-Management: Sicherstellen einer präzisen Forecasting- und Opportunity-Steuerung im CRM-System, um volle Transparenz über den Sales-Funnel zu gewährleisten. Markt- & Wettbewerbsanalyse: Beobachtung von Trends im Bereich Industrial Software & Manufacturing, um Chancen frühzeitig zu erkennen und Kunden proaktiv zu beraten. Reporting & KPI-Steuerung: Erstellung von regelmäßigen Berichten zu Sales-Performance, Conversion Rates und Forecasts für Management und Stakeholder. Dein Profil: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im B2B-Solution-Selling, idealerweise im Bereich Enterprise Software, Industrial Software oder SaaS im Fertigungsumfeld. Nachweisbare Erfolge im Closing komplexer Deals und in der Erreichung oder Übertreffung ambitionierter Vertri

This role will also contribute to the design and implementation of security strategies that align with enterprise policies, compliance requirements, and audit standards as established by the Director of Information Systems Security. The SAP Security Analyst II must demonstrate strong communication and collaboration skills, proactively partnering with business process owners, functional teams, and technical experts to ensure secure and efficient system access that supports business operations and transformation initiatives. Key Responsibilities: Lead the analysis and resolution of complex security issues involving SAP related applications, ensuring timely, compliant solutions that maintain system integrity and user productivity. Provide expert guidance on secure design principles, risk mitigation, and alignment with enterprise security architecture. Provide advanced technical expertise in SAP authorization concepts, including role and profile creation, segregation of duties (SoD) analysis, and risk mitigation. Lead the design and maintenance of complex roles supporting cross-functional and global business processes. Execute and monitor SOX and internal audit compliance activities, including user access reviews, sensitive access monitoring, and remediation of findings to ensure sustained control effectiveness. Contribute to the development and implementation of SAP security policies, standards, and procedures, ensuring alignment with corporate information security objectives and regulatory requirements. Mentor junior security analysts and specialists, providing technical guidance, reviewing deliverables for quality and consistency, and fostering continuous improvement within the SAP Security team. Required Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science / Management Information Systems preferred. May consider 8 years of relevant experience in lieu of degree. 6+ years of experience in IT, with exposure to business process

The role spans backend C#/.NET development, front-end work, content modeling, integrations, and platform upgrades, with a strong emphasis on modern composable patterns and migration from legacy implementations. The ideal candidate has shipped meaningful Sitecore work in production, navigates both classic XP/XM and the composable Sitecore ecosystem, and balances technical depth with editor and marketer needs. Key Responsibilities Design and develop Sitecore solutions across XM Cloud, XP, XM, and Content Hub. Build templates, layouts, renderings, and components following Sitecore Helix architecture principles, keeping the solution modular, upgrade-friendly, and clearly organized by feature, foundation, and project layers. Migrate legacy XP/XM implementations to XM Cloud and composable architectures. Profile and optimize performance, caching, and CDN strategies for high-traffic sites, applying systematic measurement, targeted improvements, and data-driven validation to deliver quantifiable gains in throughput, latency, or resource efficiency. Build CI/CD pipelines for Sitecore deployments using modern DevOps tooling. Participate in code reviews, contribute to standards, and document technical decisions. Troubleshoot production issues across content delivery, content management, and processing roles. Collaborate with content authors, designers, and product owners on authoring experience improvements. Stay current with Sitecore releases and composable DXP evolution, regularly review release notes and community discussions, and translate noteworthy advances into concrete recommendations and adoption proposals for the team. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field. Five or more years of Sitecore development experience. Deep expertise in C#/.NET and Sitecore APIs. Strong understanding of Sitecore Helix architecture. Hands-on experience with Sitecore XP, XM, or XM Cloud. Experience with headless implementations using JSS or Next.js. Solid front-end skills with modern JavaScript and build tools. Experience with CI/CD pipelines for Sitecore deployments. Strong troubleshooting and performance-tuning skills. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Preferred Qualifications Sitecore Certified Developer credentials. Experience with XM Cloud migrations. Familiarity with Sitecore Content Hub, CDP, or Personalize. Exposure to multi-brand or multi-region Sitecore deployments. Experience with Sitecore OrderCloud commerce integrations. jobs.title: Data Analyst - Water Infrastructure
jobs.description: About Infrasync Infrasync Technology Services helps water utilities tackle aging infrastructure, rising costs, and efficiency challenges through smart water management technology, data analytics, and engineering. The team connects a utility's physical infrastructure with its digital data systems (SCADA, CMMS, GIS, AMI), turning that data into real-time dashboards and insights instead of the long, unread engineering reports utilities have relied on for decades. Most of Infrasync's clients are municipal utilities,with active projects across Texas, Florida, Michigan, Louisiana, and other states. About the Role Infrasync's engineers design the specs behind every client dashboard — the calculations, the layout, the placeholder data. This role is about taking those specs and building them into real, working dashboards and automated reports that utility clients actually use. You'll sit right in the middle of Infrasync's workflow between engineering and the finished product, with real ownership over how the company's reporting systems evolve. Requirements Advanced proficiency with Power BI (required) including SQL, Python, and other languages Experience with Esri GIS (required). Familiarity with Microsoft Azure / Azure Fabric and API implementations. Strong ability to take complex time-series, geospatial, and asset data and turn it into data models, dashboards, and reports. jobs.description: About Reveal Technology Founded in 2019, Reveal is a dynamic startup revolutionizing field operations by delivering software tools and intelligence to operators in remote, disconnected, and extreme environments. Cloud deployment on managed Kubernetes, with reproducible builds and a strong supply-chain story. Observability end to end - traces, metrics, logs, and the dashboards that turn them into something use. Requirements 5+ years of experience building and operating production backend services with strong fundamentals in a modern systems or application language. We primarily use Go and Rust—deep expertise and a willingness to learn is ideal. Experience working with relational and non-relational databases, including Postgres; experience with graph databases and/or columnar analytical data stores is a plus. Hands-on experience deploying and operating services in Kubernetes at scale, including managed clusters, Helm charts, and troubleshooting in-cluster issues. Experience building modern web applications using React, TypeScript, Vite, and mapping libraries. Experience implementing and maintaining OpenTelemetry instrumentation beyond default configurations. Strong API and software abstraction design skills, with experience designing systems across heterogeneous backends and making thoughtful architectural trade offs. Excellent technical communication skills, including writing design documents, Architecture Decision Records (ADRs), acceptance criteria, and other engineering documentation before implementation. jobs.source_id: anatta-design-UX Researcher, Data Analyst & Optimization Specialist
jobs.title: UX Researcher, Data Analyst & Optimization Specialist
jobs.description: Overview We are looking for a passionate, curious, and self-driven UX Researcher, Data Analyst & Optimization Specialist to sit at the foundation of our optimization practice — turning raw user behavior into rigorous, decision-ready insight. This role reports directly to the UX Strategist and executes the analytical and research backbone of the optimization strategy they own. Where the Strategist sets direction and owns the client-facing narrative, this person owns the depth: the journey maps, the funnel builds, the cohort analyses, and the qualitative research that make every recommendation defensible. The ideal candidate is a true quantitative expert with equally strong qualitative instincts — someone who can pull a statistically sound insight out of a GA4 funnel and also run a usability session that explains why the number looks the way it does. You have deep comprehension of Google Analytics and the Shopify ecosystem, and you use both to tell a story around user experience through a data-driven lens. You will work cross-functionally alongside the UX Strategist, designers, and developers to properly tag events, track user flows, and evaluate site performance against defined KPIs — with a particular focus on journey mapping, funnel construction, LTV cohorting, and measuring the downstream impact of every UX change we ship. Core Responsibilities 1. Journey Mapping & Funnel Analysis • Build and maintain detailed journey maps and end-to-end conversion funnels (landing → PDP → cart → checkout → purchase → retention) • Understand and improve how users move through the funnel, diagnosing high abandonment and bounce points • Draw qualitative insight about user experience from quantitative data, and vice versa • Surface friction points and translate them into clear, testable opportunity areas for the Strategist's roadmap 2. Quantitative Data Analysis • Conduct thorough data analysis in GA4 and Shopify to understand how users interact with and move through the site • Build and maintain LTV cohort analyses to connect UX and onsite behavior to long-term customer value and retention • Own data accuracy and integrity as a core competency — analyzing, validating, and pulling insight from the numbers before anyone else touches them • Apply sound statistical practice to ensure findings are reliable, not just directional 3. Qualitative & Mixed-Methods Research • Plan and run qualitative research — usability testing, heuristic evaluation, and user interviews — to explain the “why” behind the data • Apply UX best practices and heuristics to evaluate and improve user experience • Blend qualitative and quantitative methods into a single, coherent read on user behavior • Create artifacts such as user flows, wireflows, and journey maps to communicate findings internally and to clients 4. Event Tagging & Measurement Infrastructure • Partner with developers to tag interactive elements of the site, ensuring data accuracy and full visibility into user behavior • Identify tracking gaps and help build out the event architecture that underpins the Strategist's measurement strategy • Support GA4 funnel setup, custom event design, and ongoing tracking QA 5. A/B Testing & Impact Measurement • Maintain a high-velocity, robust A/B testing program with clear strategic hypotheses tied to account goals and success metrics • Measure and report on the impact of tests and site changes on website performance, LTV, and retention • Rigorously measure the downstream impact of shipped UX implementations, not just the initial test result — closing the loop from hypothesis to long-term outcome 6. Reporting & Insight Communication • Skillfully translate key findings into insights and recommendations that support the Strategist's client narrative • Create effective, well-structured reports and dashboards that make complex data easy to act on • Present findings and recommendations clearly, both in written reports and verbal presentation to stakeholder

Identify, prioritize, and execute internal continuous improvement initiatives that scale OSM’s reach without scaling headcount. Maintain shared knowledge bases, templates, and documentation that codify how the OSM operates and onboard future team members faster. Collaborate across HR, Finance, IT, and other enterprise functions to align internal OSM operations with broader enterprise standards. Proven track record leading cross-functional initiatives without formal authority. Demonstrated people management experience — ability to set clear goals, provide consistent coaching and feedback, conduct performance reviews, and develop direct reports over time. Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into insight,

This is a 3 year term appointment which may be ended or extended based on organizational needs, funding availability, and performance. About the Cornell Experience Modernization Initiative and Donor Engagement and Experience Project The Cornell Experience Modernization Initiative (CEMI) is a multi-year program designed to improve the experience of students, faculty, staff, alumni, donors, patients, and partners by unifying and enhancing administrative systems and processes across all Cornell University locations. Its name CEMI is said as “See Me” because the focus is on you: the people who use Cornell’s systems and data to accomplish your goals. CEMI Donor Engagement and Experience Project (DEEP) is a major, multi-year effort to modernize and unify development and engagement technology across Cornell’s Ithaca and Cornell Tech campuses, Weill Cornell Medicine (WCM), and the Lab of Ornithology. What will you do: The Salesforce Platform Developer (Kindsight Ascend) plays a critical role within the Cornell Enterprise Modernization Initiative (CEMI), with primary responsibility supporting the Donor Engagement and Experience Project (DEEP)—a major, multi‑year effort to modernize and unify development and engagement technology across Cornell’s Ithaca and Cornell Tech campuses, Weill Cornell Medicine (WCM), and the Lab of Ornithology. Cornell seeks a highly skilled Salesforce Platform Developer with experience supporting Kindsight Ascend and its associated data model to contribute to a large‑scale, multicampus transformation that consolidates‑ development and engagement capabilities on a single Salesforce platform. The CEMI DEEP Salesforce Platform Developer works closely with fundraising, engagement, and operations teams across all campuses, as well as Salesforce Operations, the DEEP project team, campus stakeholders, and the system integrator responsible for Kindsight Ascend implementation. In collaboration with these partners, the developer supports and evolves the solution by extending managed packages, designing integrations, enabling data unification and marketing activation capabilities, and validating platform functionality—all while ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, security standards, and business requirements. Within Development Information Technology, the CEMI DEEP Salesforce Platform Developer contributes to Salesforce platform architecture and solution design, applying sound architectural principles, established design patterns, and enterprise governance standards. This role partners with functional and technical stakeholders to identify pain points and deliver innovative, scalable solutions that enhance the user experience while ensuring the Salesforce platform remains secure, maintainable, and aligned with Cornell’s long-term‑ technology strategy. The CEMI DEEP Salesforce Platform Developer brings deep experience working with complex Salesforce managed packages and associated data models, with Kindsight Ascend serving as the core managed package around which additional packages, integrations, and customizations are designed. The role supports enterprise fundraising, donor engagement, and reporting needs by delivering high-quality‑, scalable Salesforce solutions and integrations. Key Responsibilities Contribute to Salesforce platform architecture and solution design, including data architecture, integration strategy, environment design, and release management approaches. Design and implement scalable Salesforce solutions using sound architectural principles, enterprise design patterns, and platform governance standards. Develop and maintain Salesforce applications, custom components, and workflow logic using Apex, Lightning Web Components (LWC), Flows, and declarative automation. Solution optimization: Design and maintain complex configurations (cost centers, intercompany billing, allocations, revenue recognition/allocation as applicable); configure, test, validate, and deploy Business Process updates. Release management: Evaluate and adopt Workday feature releases; run impact assessments, regression testing, and stakeholder communications. Innovation and AI: Champion Workday’s innovation roadmap; evaluate AI/ML features for reconciliation, anomaly detection, and forecasting in line with Kyriba Finance objectives. Analytics: Enable reporting and dashboards using Workday Report Writer/Composite, Prism Analytics, and Adaptive Planning to provide real-time, multi-dimensional insight. Governance, security, and data: Own finance master data stewardship (values, validation rules, exception reporting); role-based security, and audit evidence processes with IT Security and Internal Audit. Partnerships and mentorship: Serve as escalation point for Finance Systems and Finance Ops; coach team members and drive continuous learning. Knowledge, skills, and abilities Workday expertise: Deep functional knowledge of Core Financials (GL, AP, AR), Expenses, and Procurement; hands-on configuration and reporting experience. Finance operations: Solid understanding of US GAAP/IFRS c

In this role you will be responsible for ingesting, transforming, and analyzing massive volumes of structured and unstructured data to support enterprise analytics, machine learning, and reporting workloads. Automate pipeline orchestration using Airflow, Oozie, or similar workflow engines, with clean dependency management and clear ownership boundaries. Continuously evaluate and adopt new technologies in the big-data and cloud ecosystem (EMR, Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) where they offer meaningful improvements. Lead performance reviews and architecture audits of existing pipelines, proposing concrete refactoring and optimization initiatives. Document data architectures, schemas, pipeline behaviors, and operational runbooks in a way that makes the platform supportable as the team scales. Mentor junior engineers and contribute to the team’s engineering standards and best practices. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline. Solid experience with Hive, HDFS, Sqoop, HBase, and the broader Hadoop ecosystem. Hands-on experience with streaming data platforms such as Kafka, Spark Streaming, or Flink. Strong SQL skills and experience working with both relational and NoSQL data stores. Experience with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow or Oozie.
